task_completion.description: Open the target image in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The landscape image is visible in the GIMP workspace.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to change the image's print resolution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Set Image Print Resolution' (Image -> Print Size) or 'Scale Image' dialog is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the X and Y resolution to 300 DPI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the resolution dialog, the X and Y resolution fields are set to 300 pixels/in.

task_completion.description: Ensure the pixel dimensions remain unchanged.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dialog shows the pixel dimensions are still 1200x800 (or the physical print size changes while pixel dimensions are explicitly untouched).

task_completion.description: Save or export the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' or 'Save' dialog is visible, or there is evidence of the file being successfully saved (e.g., saving as 'landscape_300dpi.jpg').

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to modify the resolution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent did not resample or scale the actual pixels of the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The pixel dimensions (1200x800) are never altered in the Scale Image dialog.

integrity.description: The image is exported using GIMP's native export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP 'Export Image' dialog is used, rather than a script or external tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a 1200x800 pixel JPEG with a default 72 DPI. The target is to change the metadata to 300 DPI (±10 tolerance) while keeping the pixel dimensions exactly 1200x800. In GIMP, this is typically done via 'Image -> Print Size' which alters DPI without resampling pixels. The expected export filename is 'landscape_300dpi.jpg'.
